responsibilities: Develop an AI roadmap aligned with OEM requirements, engineering needs, and plant KPIs.
Evaluate emerging AI technologies relevant to automotive quality, traceability, and engineering workflows.
Lead end to end development of AI applications including: Computer vision for defect detection, weld inspection, assembly verification, and surface quality.
Oversee data acquisition from sensors, PLCs, MES, SCADA, and engineering systems.
Ensure seamless integration of AI tools with ERP, MES, QMS, PLM, CAD/CAE, and traceability platforms.
Collaborate with product engineering teams to embed AI into the product development lifecycle.
Support engineering systems such as: PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) CAD/CAE tools (e.g., CATIA, NX, SolidWorks, ANSYS)
Ensure AI tools support IATF 16949, APQP, PPAP, FMEA, MSA, and OEM specific quality requirements.

companyResearch.cultureNotes: Our diverse and multicultural workplaces bring minds and talents together from across the world into a strong network of innovators and inventors. The automotive industry has limitless opportunities for developing ideas into reality, and we always encourage new concepts to be driven forward. If you have a passion for being a part of new ideas, are driven to excel in your field, and thrive in a supportive environment where inclusion, diversity and creative thinking are embraced - check out our career pages to find your fit with us!
